item.title: Should I interrupt it?
item.description: Few days ago, while going through some literature on architectural details of a computer, I came across very interesting claim to prefer to use polling instead of going for interrupt in case of NICs in modern systems. As with anything that have gone through lot of advances to squeeze every little bit of performance and utility, microarchitectures too do exhibit some complications accumulated through the decades of experimentation and research

item.title: About
item.description:
I am a pixelated dehumanized being Oops!

Hello! I am Deepak Sharma, a Computer Science and Engineering sophomore at Amrapali Institute of Technology and Sciences. I have a great fascination with understanding the complexities of various subjects and aesthetics (and wasting my time procrastinating)

I have had great deal of fun in low level/bare metal programming. I am currenly working on increasing the bounds of my knowledge through literature and occasionally attempting to code some cool stuff

My main interests include :

  • Computer systems (compilers, OS and computer architecture)
  • Computer graphics and generative art
  • I also love me some math (tho not really great at it)
  • Analytical philosophy

Some leisure activites include jamming some Japanese video game soundtracks or some gems from European indie music scene, and just randomly reading about stuffs
